name: init-deep
tagline: Spawns parallel agents + LSP/codegraph analysis to generate scored, hierarchical AGENTS.md documentation files.
verdict: No malicious behavior found: purely local exploration/doc generation, no secrets, network egress, or remote code execution. Real risks are quality gaps, not safety ones: --create-new deletes existing AGENTS.md files on just a CLI flag, description lacks explicit use/avoid triggers, and it silently assumes several harness-specific tools (multi_agent_v1, codeg

highlights.summary: '--create-new: Read existing first → then delete all → regenerate' (line ~139) is an irreversible bulk delete gated only by a CLI flag, with no explicit confirmation step inside the skill itself.
Description '(builtin) Initialize hierarchical AGENTS.md knowledge base' states what it does but not an explicit 'use when' condition (e.g. new repo, post-refactor).
No negative-trigger guidance anywhere in the file (e.g. skip for trivial/single-file repos, or when AGENTS.md is manually curated).
